walkthrough.title: Place the Board and Token Trays
walkthrough.text: Let's get PARKS set up. First, unfold the tri-fold board and place it in the center of the table where everyone can reach it. Now grab the two token trays. Each tray holds all types of tokens. Place one tray on the left side of the board and one on the right. Remove the tray lids and return them to the box. Fill the trays with the resource tokens: 15 Forest tokens, 15 Mountain tokens, 30 Sunshine tokens, 30 Water tokens, and 12 Wildlife tokens. Also place the 28 Photos in the trays. These trays form the shared supply for the entire game.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Parks Area
walkthrough.text: Now let's fill the board with Park cards. Find the large tarot-sized Park cards — there are 48 of them. Shuffle them all face down into a deck. Deal 3 Park cards face up into the top slots on the board. Place the remaining Parks deck face down onto the Parks deck area on the top right of the board. These are the national parks you will be visiting during the game to score points.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Gear Area
walkthrough.text: Next, find the 36 Gear cards. These are the smaller cards that give you ongoing abilities during the game. Shuffle them face down into a Gear deck. Deal 3 Gear cards face up into the bottom Gear slots on the board. Place the remaining Gear deck face down onto the Gear deck area on the bottom right of the board. Gear cards cost Sunshine tokens to buy and supplement your hiking journey with powerful ongoing benefits.

walkthrough.title: Deal Canteen Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the 15 Canteen cards and shuffle them face down. Deal 1 Canteen card face up to each player. This is each player's starting Canteen. Canteens give you a bonus action when you fill them with Water during the game. Place the remaining Canteen deck face down onto its area on the top left of the board.

walkthrough.title: Deal Year Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the 12 Year cards and shuffle them face down. Deal 2 Year cards to each player. Each player secretly looks at their two cards and chooses one as their Personal Bonus for the Year, then discards the other face down. Year cards award 2 or 3 bonus points at game end based on a personal goal — like visiting Parks with certain symbols or taking a specific number of Photos. Keep your chosen Year card face down until final scoring. For your very first game, just deal 1 Year card to each player instead of 2. Return any unused Year cards face down to the box.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Season Deck
walkthrough.text: Find the 10 Season cards and shuffle them into a face-down deck. Place it onto the Season area of the board. Reveal the top card — this is the first Season of the Year. The Season card has two important pieces of information: a Season Effect that changes gameplay for the round, and a Weather Pattern that determines which bonus tokens appear on the Trail.

walkthrough.title: Build the Trail
walkthrough.text: Now for the most important part of setup — building the Trail. Place the Trailhead tile on the far left below the board. Separate the 10 Trail Site tiles into two groups: the 5 Basic Sites (Forest, Mountain, Valley, Ocean, and Vista) and the 4 Advanced Sites (marked with an asterisk in the bottom right corner). If you are playing with 4 or 5 players, also add the Waterfall Basic Site — otherwise return it to the box. Shuffle the 4 Advanced Site tiles face down and add 1 random Advanced Site to the Basic Sites to form the Trail deck. Place the remaining 3 Advanced Sites face down to the left of the Trailhead for future Seasons. Shuffle the Trail deck and reveal the Sites one at a time, placing each to the right of the previous one. Finally, place the Trail End tile to the right of the last Site. Make sure Trail End is not on its SOLO side. The first Season's Trail is now formed.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Player Colors
walkthrough.text: Each player chooses a color and takes 2 Hikers of that color. Place both Hikers on the Trailhead. Each player also takes 1 Campfire token matching their color — place it on the lit side in front of you. The Campfire lets you share a Trail Site with another Hiker. Give the First Hiker Marker (the enamel pin) to the player who most recently took a hike in real life. Give the Camera to the player sitting to the right of the First Hiker. Return any unused Hikers and Campfires to the box.

walkthrough.title: Apply the Weather Pattern
walkthrough.text: Look at the revealed Season card. Find the Weather Pattern displayed on the bottom right of the card. Starting with the 2nd Trail Site (not the 1st), place the indicated tokens from the supply onto the Trail — one token per Site — from left to right. Simply repeat the Weather Pattern down the Trail until you reach the Trail End. Do not place any token on the Trailhead, the 1st Trail Site, or the Trail End itself. The Weather tokens are bonus resources that Hikers collect when they are the first to land on that Site. You are ready to begin the first Season!

summary.title: Place the Board and Token Trays
bullets: Unfold tri-fold board centrally
Place 2 token trays (left and right of board) with all token types
Supply: 15 Forest, 15 Mountain, 30 Sunshine, 30 Water, 12 Wildlife, 28 Photos

summary.title: Set Up the Parks Area
bullets: Shuffle all 48 Park cards face down
Deal 3 face up into the top board slots
Place Parks deck on top-right deck area

summary.title: Set Up the Gear Area
bullets: Shuffle all 36 Gear cards face down
Deal 3 face up into the bottom board slots
Place Gear deck on bottom-right deck area

summary.title: Deal Canteen Cards
bullets: Shuffle 15 Canteen cards face down
Deal 1 face up to each player (starting Canteen)
Place remaining Canteen deck on top-left board area

summary.title: Deal Year Cards
bullets: Shuffle 12 Year cards, deal 2 to each player
Each player keeps 1 as their secret Personal Bonus, discards the other face down
Year cards award 2-3 bonus points at game end
First game variant: deal only 1 Year card per player

summary.title: Set Up the Season Deck
bullets: Shuffle 10 Season cards into a face-down deck
Place on Season area of board
Reveal top card as the first Season (shows Season Effect + Weather Pattern)

summary.title: Build the Trail
bullets: Place Trailhead on far left below the board
Separate 5 Basic Sites (Forest, Mountain, Valley, Ocean, Vista) from 4 Advanced Sites (asterisk)
4-5 players: add Waterfall to Basic Sites
Shuffle Advanced Sites, add 1 to Basics to form Trail deck; set aside remaining 3 Advanced Sites
Reveal and place Sites left to right, then place Trail End on the far right

summary.title: Set Up Player Colors
bullets: Each player takes 2 Hikers of their chosen color and places them on the Trailhead
Each player takes 1 matching Campfire (lit side up)
First Hiker Marker to the most recent real-life hiker
Camera to the player to the right of the First Hiker

summary.title: Apply the Weather Pattern
bullets: Read the Weather Pattern from the Season card
Starting at the 2nd Trail Site, place 1 token per Site from left to right
Repeat the pattern until Trail End (no tokens on Trailhead, 1st Site, or Trail End)
Weather tokens are bonus resources for the first Hiker to land on each Site

walkthrough.title: The Four Seasons
walkthrough.text: PARKS is played over 4 Seasons, which form the 4 rounds of the game. Each Season, all players send their 2 Hikers down the Trail, visiting Sites and collecting resources along the way. When all Hikers reach the Trail End, the Season ends. As each new Season begins, the Trail grows one Site longer because a new Advanced Site is added. So the Trail gets longer and offers more options each round. Your goal is to collect the most points from visited Parks, Photos, and your Personal Bonus Year card by the end of the fourth Season.

walkthrough.title: Taking a Turn
walkthrough.text: The player with the First Hiker Marker takes the first turn of each Season. On your turn, choose one of your two Hikers and move it forward down the Trail to any unoccupied Site to the right of that Hiker's current position. You can skip over Sites — you do not have to move to the next one. When your Hiker lands on a Site, you perform that Site's action. Then the next player clockwise takes their turn. Turns continue around the table until the Season ends. Important: you must be able to perform a Site's action in order to visit it.

walkthrough.title: Weather Tokens on the Trail
walkthrough.text: Remember those Weather tokens placed during setup? When a Hiker is the first to land on a Site that has a Weather token, they collect that token into their personal supply as a bonus — in addition to performing the Site's action. Weather tokens can even be used immediately toward that Site's action. For example, if you land on the Lodge and pick up a Weather token, you can use that token as part of the Lodge's exchange. Once collected, Weather tokens are gone for the Season.

walkthrough.title: Campfires and Sharing Sites
walkthrough.text: Normally, if another Hiker — even your own — is already occupying a Site, you cannot visit it. To share a Site that is already occupied, you must use your Campfire by flipping it to the extinguished side. Once extinguished, you cannot share Sites again until one of your Hikers reaches Trail End, which relights it automatically. Use your Campfire wisely — it is a one-time-per-Season ability (until relit) and can be the difference between reaching a critical Site or being blocked out.

walkthrough.title: Basic Trail Sites
walkthrough.text: There are 5 Basic Trail Sites, plus the Waterfall in 4-5 player games. Forest: take 1 Forest token. Mountain: take 1 Mountain token. Valley: take 2 Sunshine tokens. Ocean: take 2 Water tokens. Vista: draw a Canteen card OR turn in 2 tokens to take a Photo and gain the Camera. Waterfall (4+ players only): gain 1 Sunshine and 1 Water token. Each Site gives you resources you will need to visit Parks or buy Gear later.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Trail Sites
walkthrough.text: There are 4 Advanced Sites, marked with an asterisk. Wildlife: turn in any 1 token to gain a Wildlife token. Lodge: turn in 2 tokens and gain any other 2 tokens (excluding Wildlife) — the tokens you gain cannot match the ones you turned in. Lookout: reserve or visit a Park, OR buy a Gear card (same actions available at Trail End). River: spend 1 Water to copy the action of another Site that is currently occupied by any Hiker. One new Advanced Site is added to the Trail each Season, making later Seasons more strategic.

walkthrough.title: Wildlife Tokens
walkthrough.text: Wildlife tokens come in 12 different shapes, but they all function identically. Wildlife is the most flexible resource in the game — it can be used in place of any other token type when visiting Parks or taking Photos. However, Wildlife cannot be used to fill Canteens or to buy Gear. Because Wildlife is so versatile, the Wildlife Trail Site (turn in 1 token to gain 1 Wildlife) is extremely valuable, especially when you need a token type that is hard to find on the current Trail.

walkthrough.title: Token Limit
walkthrough.text: Players can carry up to 12 tokens at any time. If you have more than 12 tokens at the end of your turn, you must discard down to 12. This limit forces you to spend resources rather than hoarding indefinitely. Plan your token management carefully — visit Parks or buy Gear before your supply overflows.

walkthrough.title: Canteens
walkthrough.text: Canteen cards give you bonus actions when filled with Water. Each Canteen shows a specific benefit — like gaining a token or taking an extra action. To fill a Canteen, you must place a Water token that you gained on that same turn directly onto the Canteen instead of into your personal supply. You cannot use Water you already had from a previous turn. You can have any number of Canteens, and multiple Canteens can be filled on the same turn if you gain multiple Water tokens. Once filled, the Water stays on the Canteen until the end of the Season and cannot be spent elsewhere.

walkthrough.title: Photos and the Camera
walkthrough.text: Photos are worth 1 point each at game end. To take a Photo at the Vista, return any 2 tokens from your supply to the general supply. After taking the Photo, gain the Camera from whoever currently has it. While you hold the Camera, Photos only cost 1 token instead of 2. At the end of each Season, the player holding the Camera may take one Photo at the reduced cost. Some Gear cards also let you take Photos outside of the Vista, but those do not give you the Camera.

walkthrough.title: Trail End Actions
walkthrough.text: When a Hiker reaches Trail End, you immediately relight your Campfire if extinguished, then choose one of three areas. Reserve a Park: choose 1 of the 3 face-up Parks or draw from the top of the Parks deck. Place it face up horizontally in front of you — it is reserved for a future visit. The first player to reach Trail End and Reserve a Park also gains the First Hiker Marker, which is worth 1 point at game end. Buy Gear: choose 1 of the 3 available Gear cards and pay its Sunshine cost. In 3 or fewer player games, the first Hiker to buy Gear gets a 1-Sunshine discount. In 4+ player games, the first 2 Hikers each get the discount. Visit a Park: choose 1 of the 3 available Parks or one of your reserved Parks and pay its token cost. Place the visited Park vertically in your score area.

walkthrough.title: Gear Cards
walkthrough.text: Gear cards cost Sunshine tokens to buy and provide ongoing abilities for the rest of the game. Some also have an instant one-time action when purchased, marked on the card. Ongoing abilities on Gear include things like discounting Park visits by specific token types (Maps, Compasses, Journals), swapping token types when visiting Parks (Sunscreen, Rain Gear), or gaining free Canteen fills at certain Sites (Water Bottle). Multiple Gear card discounts can stack on a single Park visit, but a token type on a Park can never be reduced below zero.

walkthrough.title: Last Hiker on the Trail
walkthrough.text: Once both of a player's Hikers reach Trail End, that player takes no more turns for the Season. When only one Hiker remains on the entire Trail, that player must move their Hiker directly to Trail End and choose an action there. This ends the Season. Use this strategically — if you get both Hikers to Trail End quickly, you can force an opponent's remaining Hiker to skip straight to the end, denying them Sites they wanted to visit.

walkthrough.title: End of a Season
walkthrough.text: When the Season ends, the player holding the Camera may take a Photo at the reduced 1-token cost. Then perform these steps in order: 1) Empty all Canteens by returning their Water tokens to the supply. 2) Return all Hikers to the Trailhead. 3) Pick up all Trail Sites except the Trailhead and Trail End. Add 1 new Advanced Site from the face-down pile. Shuffle all the Sites together and lay out the new, longer Trail. 4) Put the previous Season card on the bottom of the Season deck and reveal the new Season from the top. Apply its Weather Pattern to the Trail. 5) The player with the First Hiker Marker takes the first turn of the new Season.

walkthrough.title: Game End and Scoring
walkthrough.text: The game ends when the fourth Season concludes, including the final Camera Photo opportunity. All players reveal their Year cards. Score points from three sources: 1) Park cards — add up the point values on all Parks you visited. 2) Photos — each Photo is worth 1 point. 3) Personal Bonus — your Year card awards 2 or 3 points based on whether you met its goal. The First Hiker Marker is also worth 1 point to whoever holds it. The player with the most total points wins. If tied, the player who visited the most Parks wins. If still tied, the victory is shared.

summary.title: The Four Seasons
bullets: 4 Seasons = 4 rounds; each Season ends when all Hikers reach Trail End
Trail grows 1 Site longer each Season (new Advanced Site added)
Goal: most points from Parks + Photos + Personal Bonus Year card

You must be able to perform a Site's action to visit it

Mountain: gain 1 Mountain token
Valley: gain 2 Sunshine tokens

bullets: 12 unique shapes, all function identically
Wild: substitutes for any token when visiting Parks or taking Photos
Cannot be used in Canteens or toward buying Gear

bullets: Maximum 12 tokens at end of your turn
Discard down to 12 if over the limit

summary.title: Canteens

Filled Canteen triggers its bonus action immediately
Water stays on Canteen until end of Season (cannot be spent elsewhere)
Multiple Canteens can be filled on the same turn

summary.title: Photos and the Camera
bullets: Photos: 1 point each at game end
Vista Photo cost: 2 tokens (or 1 token if you hold the Camera)
Taking a Photo at the Vista gives you the Camera

summary.title: Trail End Actions
bullets: Arriving relights your Campfire automatically
Reserve a Park: take 1 of 3 face-up Parks or draw from deck (first player also gains First Hiker Marker)
Buy Gear: pay Sunshine cost (first Hiker gets 1-Sunshine discount in 3- player games; first 2 in 4+ games)

bullets: Cost Sunshine to buy; provide ongoing abilities for the rest of the game
Some have an instant one-time action on purchase

Examples: Maps, Compasses, Journals (discounts); Sunscreen, Rain Gear (token swaps)

summary.title: Last Hiker on the Trail
bullets: When only 1 Hiker remains on the Trail, that player must move directly to Trail End
This ends the Season — use it strategically to cut opponents short

bullets: Camera holder may take 1 Photo at reduced cost
Empty all Canteens (return Water to supply)
Return Hikers to Trailhead; rebuild Trail with 1 new Advanced Site (Trail grows longer)
Reveal new Season card and apply its Weather Pattern
First Hiker Marker holder takes the first turn

summary.title: Game End and Scoring
bullets: Game ends after the 4th Season's Camera Photo opportunity
Score: Park card points + Photos (1 pt each) + Year card Personal Bonus (2-3 pts)
First Hiker Marker = 1 bonus point
Tiebreaker: most Parks visited, then shared victory

walkthrough.title: Welcome and Recommended Setup
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first Season with 3 players — Player 1 (green), Player 2 (pink), and Player 3 (blue). Three players is the recommended count for PARKS. Each player has 2 Hikers on the Trailhead, a lit Campfire, a starting Canteen, and a face-down Year card. The Trail has the Trailhead, 6 Sites (5 Basic plus 1 Advanced), and the Trail End. Weather tokens have been placed according to the Season card. Player 1 has the First Hiker Marker. Player 2 has the Camera. Let's hike!

walkthrough.title: Understanding the Trail Layout
walkthrough.text: Looking at our Trail from left to right: Trailhead, then the Forest Site, Mountain Site (with a Sunshine Weather token), Valley Site (with a Water Weather token), Wildlife Site, Ocean Site (with a Sunshine Weather token), Vista Site (with a Water Weather token), and finally Trail End. Remember, both Hikers start on the Trailhead. You can move either Hiker to any unoccupied Site to the right of its current position. You do not have to go in order — you can skip Sites.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's First Turn: Collecting Resources
walkthrough.text: Player 1 (green) goes first because they hold the First Hiker Marker. Green moves one of their Hikers to the Mountain Site. They gain 1 Mountain token from the supply. Since this is the first Hiker to land here, Green also collects the Sunshine Weather token as a bonus. A great opening — 2 tokens in one move. Green now has 1 Mountain and 1 Sunshine.

walkthrough.title: Player 2's First Turn: Leapfrogging
walkthrough.text: Player 2 (pink) notices the Valley Site has a Water Weather token on it. Pink moves a Hiker past the Forest Site all the way to the Valley. They gain 2 Sunshine tokens from the Valley's action and also collect the Water Weather token. Pink now has 2 Sunshine and 1 Water — and the Water could potentially fill their starting Canteen right now since it was gained this turn.

walkthrough.title: Filling a Canteen
walkthrough.text: Player 2 decides to fill their starting Canteen with the Water token they just collected. Instead of placing the Water into their personal supply, they place it directly onto the Canteen card and immediately resolve the Canteen's bonus action. Nice — that is exactly how Canteens work. Remember, you can only fill a Canteen with Water gained on that same turn, not Water you had from before.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's First Turn
walkthrough.text: Player 3 (blue) wants the Forest token. Blue moves a Hiker to the Forest Site and gains 1 Forest token. Simple and effective — Forest tokens are needed to visit many Parks. The Forest Site did not have a Weather token, so no bonus this time.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's Mistake: Forgetting the Campfire
walkthrough.text: Player 1 (green) wants to visit the Valley for those 2 Sunshine tokens. But wait — Player 2's Hiker is already sitting on the Valley. Green has their Campfire lit, so they could flip it to share the Site. But Green decides to save their Campfire and moves to the Wildlife Site instead. At the Wildlife Site, Green must turn in 1 token to gain 1 Wildlife token. Green turns in their Mountain token. Now Green has 1 Sunshine and 1 Wildlife. Was skipping the Valley the right call? Let's see what happens.

walkthrough.title: Player 2's Mistake: Moving Too Fast
walkthrough.text: Player 2 (pink) is eager to reach Trail End first. Pink moves their first Hiker all the way to the Vista, skipping the Ocean Site entirely. At the Vista, Pink has a choice: draw a Canteen or spend 2 tokens for a Photo. Pink already has the Camera (from setup), so Photos only cost 1 token. Pink spends 1 Sunshine token and takes a Photo. Good move getting a cheap Photo! But by racing ahead, Pink skipped the Ocean Site (2 Water tokens) and its Sunshine Weather token. That is 3 tokens worth of resources Pink will never get from this Hiker.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Don't Rush the Trail
walkthrough.text: The temptation to rush to Trail End is real — the First Hiker Marker and early Park reservations are valuable. But every Site you skip is resources you will never collect. Two Water from the Ocean plus a Weather token is worth more than arriving at Trail End one turn earlier in most situations. Move your Hikers at a steady pace, collecting resources from as many Sites as possible. Use your second Hiker to cover Sites your first Hiker missed.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's Mistake: Wasting the Campfire
walkthrough.text: Player 3 (blue) sees that the Mountain Site has no Weather token left (Green collected it earlier), but Blue wants a Mountain token. The Mountain Site is empty — no Hiker is occupying it. Blue flips their Campfire to the extinguished side and moves their Hiker there. But wait — the Mountain was unoccupied! Blue did not need to use their Campfire at all. The Campfire is only required when another Hiker is already on the Site you want to visit.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Only Use Your Campfire When Blocked
walkthrough.text: Your Campfire is precious — you only get to use it once per Season before it needs relighting at Trail End. Never flip it unless another Hiker is physically occupying the Site you want. Check the Trail carefully before deciding. Now Player 3's Campfire is extinguished, meaning if they need to share a Site later this Season, they are out of luck.

walkthrough.title: Continuing the Season
walkthrough.text: Play continues clockwise. Player 1 moves their second Hiker to the Ocean Site, gaining 2 Water tokens and the Sunshine Weather token. Player 2 moves their second Hiker to the Forest to grab a Forest token. Player 3 moves their first Hiker forward to the Ocean — but Player 1 is there! With an extinguished Campfire, Blue cannot visit and must choose a different Site. Blue moves past to the Vista and draws a Canteen card instead.

walkthrough.title: Arriving at Trail End
walkthrough.text: As Hikers reach Trail End, each player relights their Campfire (if extinguished) and chooses one of three actions: Reserve a Park, Buy Gear, or Visit a Park. Player 2's first Hiker arrives at Trail End. Pink relights the Campfire. Pink has enough tokens to visit a Park — let's say the face-up Park costs 1 Forest and 1 Mountain. Pink checks their supply. Oops — Pink skipped the Forest and Mountain Sites in their rush. Pink does not have those token types. Pink Reserves a Park for later instead.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Match Your Resources to Available Parks
walkthrough.text: Before racing down the Trail, glance at the 3 available Park cards. Each Park shows which tokens it costs to visit — combinations of Forest, Mountain, Sunshine, and Water. Plan your Trail movement to collect the tokens you actually need. There is no point in hoarding Sunshine if every available Park costs Forest and Water. Also keep an eye on the Gear cards — their Sunshine costs affect whether you should stockpile Sunshine or spend it on Parks.

walkthrough.title: First Hiker Marker and Gear Discounts
walkthrough.text: Player 1's Hiker reaches Trail End and chooses to Reserve a Park. Since Player 2 already chose Reserve a Park, the First Hiker Marker slot is taken — Player 1 places their Hiker in the next available slot and does not gain the First Hiker Marker. The Marker already went to the first Hiker who chose that area. Meanwhile, Player 3's Hiker reaches Trail End and decides to Buy Gear. As the first player to buy Gear this Season, Blue gets a 1-Sunshine discount. Blue picks a Gear card that discounts future Park visits — a great investment for future Seasons.

walkthrough.title: Ending the Season
walkthrough.text: Once all 6 Hikers are at Trail End, the Season is over. The Camera holder (Player 2) gets the chance to take a Photo at the reduced 1-token cost. Then: empty all Canteens, return Hikers to the Trailhead, pick up all Trail Sites, add 1 new Advanced Site, shuffle, and lay out the new, longer Trail. Reveal the next Season card and apply its Weather Pattern. The player holding the First Hiker Marker starts the next Season.

walkthrough.title: Season 1 Recap
walkthrough.text: Let's review what happened. Player 1 played conservatively and collected resources from multiple Sites — a solid haul of tokens and a reserved Park. Player 2 rushed the Trail and got a cheap Photo with the Camera, but missed critical resource Sites and couldn't afford to visit a Park. Player 3 wasted their Campfire on an unoccupied Site and got blocked from the Ocean later. The lessons: collect resources steadily, match your tokens to available Parks, and only use your Campfire when truly blocked.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen all the core actions in PARKS — moving Hikers, collecting resources, filling Canteens, taking Photos, using the Campfire, and choosing Trail End actions. You have also seen three common mistakes: rushing past valuable Sites, wasting the Campfire on empty Sites, and failing to match token collection to Park costs. The Trail gets longer and more interesting each Season as new Advanced Sites appear. Now you are ready to play a full game.

walkthrough.title: Before You Hit the Trail
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First — check out General Tips for strategy basics on Trail pacing, Campfire timing, and Gear investment.

Second — reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every Trail Site, token type, and game term.

And most importantly — ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Happy hiking!

summary.title: Welcome and Recommended Setup
bullets: Scripted first Season with 3 players (green, pink, blue) — recommended player count
Each player has 2 Hikers, 1 Campfire (lit), 1 starting Canteen, 1 Year card
Trail: Trailhead + 6 Sites (5 Basic, 1 Advanced) + Trail End with Weather tokens placed

summary.title: Understanding the Trail Layout
bullets: Trail runs left to right: Trailhead -> Forest -> Mountain -> Valley -> Wildlife -> Ocean -> Vista -> Trail End
Weather tokens on Mountain (Sunshine), Valley (Water), Ocean (Sunshine), Vista (Water)
Both Hikers start on the Trailhead; move to any unoccupied Site to the right

summary.title: Player 1's First Turn: Collecting Resources
bullets: Green moves Hiker to Mountain: gains 1 Mountain token
First to land, so also collects the Sunshine Weather token
Result: 1 Mountain + 1 Sunshine from a single move

summary.title: Player 2's First Turn: Leapfrogging
bullets: Pink skips Forest and moves to Valley: gains 2 Sunshine
Collects Water Weather token as bonus
Skipping Sites is legal — you can jump ahead to any unoccupied Site

summary.title: Filling a Canteen
bullets: Pink fills starting Canteen with the Water gained this turn
Place Water on Canteen instead of into supply, resolve bonus action
Canteens can only be filled with Water gained on that same turn

summary.title: Player 3's First Turn
bullets: Blue moves to Forest: gains 1 Forest token
No Weather token on this Site — no bonus
Forest tokens are needed for many Park visits

summary.title: Player 1's Mistake: Forgetting the Campfire
bullets: Green wants Valley (occupied by Pink) but saves Campfire
Moves to Wildlife instead: turns in 1 Mountain for 1 Wildlife
Sometimes using the Campfire early is the right play

summary.title: Player 2's Mistake: Moving Too Fast
bullets: Pink skips Ocean (2 Water + Weather token) to reach Vista quickly
Takes a cheap Photo with the Camera (1 token) — good
But missed 3 tokens of resources by skipping — bad tradeoff

summary.title: Lesson: Don't Rush the Trail
bullets: Every skipped Site is resources you will never collect
Use both Hikers to cover different stretches of the Trail
Pace yourself — resources now fund Parks later

summary.title: Player 3's Mistake: Wasting the Campfire
bullets: Blue flips Campfire to visit Mountain — but it was unoccupied
Campfire only needed when another Hiker is on the Site
Now Blue cannot share any occupied Site for the rest of the Season

summary.title: Lesson: Only Use Your Campfire When Blocked
bullets: Campfire is once per Season (relights at Trail End)
Check if a Site is occupied before flipping
Wasting it means you are locked out of occupied Sites later

summary.title: Continuing the Season
bullets: Players continue moving second Hikers to collect remaining resources
Player 3's extinguished Campfire blocks them from an occupied Site
Consequence of the wasted Campfire plays out immediately

summary.title: Arriving at Trail End
bullets: Trail End relights your Campfire automatically
Choose: Reserve a Park, Buy Gear, or Visit a Park
Player 2 cannot afford any Park — forced to Reserve instead

summary.title: Lesson: Match Your Resources to Available Parks
bullets: Check the 3 face-up Park costs before planning your Trail movement
Collect the token types you actually need for those Parks
No point hoarding tokens that do not match any available Park

summary.title: First Hiker Marker and Gear Discounts
bullets: First to Reserve a Park gains the First Hiker Marker (1 point at game end)
First to Buy Gear gets a 1-Sunshine discount (3 or fewer players)
Plan Trail End timing to grab these bonuses

summary.title: Ending the Season
bullets: Camera holder takes optional Photo at reduced cost
Empty Canteens, return Hikers, rebuild Trail (now 1 Site longer)
Reveal new Season, apply Weather Pattern, First Hiker starts next Season

summary.title: Season 1 Recap
bullets: Player 1: steady resource collection, solid position
Player 2: rushed Trail, got Photo but missed resources and could not visit a Park
Player 3: wasted Campfire, got blocked later in the Season

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: You have seen all core actions: moving, collecting, Canteens, Photos, Campfire, Trail End
Three common mistakes: rushing the Trail, wasting Campfire, mismatching tokens to Parks
Trail grows longer each Season with new Advanced Sites

summary.title: Before You Hit the Trail
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics
Reference the Appendix for Trail Sites, tokens, and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide — tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the biggest mistakes new PARKS players make. First, rushing down the Trail and skipping resource Sites — every Site you skip is tokens you will never collect. Second, wasting your Campfire on an unoccupied Site or using it too early when you might need it more later. Third, hoarding tokens without visiting Parks — tokens sitting in your supply are not points. Fourth, ignoring the available Park cards and collecting random tokens that do not match any Park's cost. Fifth, forgetting the 12-token limit and being forced to discard. Keep these in mind and you will avoid the biggest beginner traps.

walkthrough.title: Trail Pacing
walkthrough.text: PARKS rewards steady movement, not speed. You have 2 Hikers, and together they should cover as many Sites on the Trail as possible. A good pattern is to send one Hiker ahead to claim key Sites while using the second Hiker to fill in the gaps. If a valuable Site has a Weather token on it, prioritize being the first to land there — that bonus token is free. In later Seasons, the Trail is longer (up to 9 Sites by Season 4), so you have more room to spread your Hikers and collect more resources.

walkthrough.title: Campfire Management
walkthrough.text: Your Campfire is one of the most important tactical tools in the game. It lets you share a Site occupied by another Hiker — including your own Hiker. Once used, it stays extinguished until one of your Hikers reaches Trail End. The best time to use your Campfire is when there is a high-value Site you absolutely need — like the Wildlife Site when you need a wild token, or the Vista when you want a cheap Photo. Do not burn it early on a low-value Site. In 2-player games, blocking is lighter so you may rarely need it. In 4-5 player games, the Trail gets crowded fast and Campfire timing is critical.

walkthrough.title: Resource Planning
walkthrough.text: Before you start moving Hikers, look at the 3 face-up Park cards and note what tokens they require. If the available Parks need Forest and Water tokens, plan your Trail movement to hit the Forest and Ocean Sites. Also check your Year card — if it rewards visiting Parks with certain symbols, focus on Parks and tokens that advance that goal. Wildlife tokens are incredibly flexible since they substitute for any token type when visiting Parks. If you are one token short, the Wildlife Site or a well-timed Lodge trade can save your Season.

walkthrough.title: Gear Investment
walkthrough.text: Gear cards cost Sunshine to buy, but their ongoing abilities pay dividends every Season for the rest of the game. The earlier you buy Gear, the more value you get. Discount Gear cards like Maps, Compasses, and Journals reduce the cost of visiting Parks — making expensive Parks affordable. Token-swap Gear like Sunscreen and Rain Gear give you flexibility to visit Parks you otherwise could not afford. The first Hiker to buy Gear each Season gets a 1-Sunshine discount, so try to time your Trail End arrival to grab that bonus. Investing 2-3 Sunshine in Gear during Season 1 or 2 can save you 6-8 tokens over the rest of the game.

walkthrough.title: The Camera and Photo Strategy
walkthrough.text: Photos are worth 1 point each, and the Camera reduces their cost from 2 tokens to 1. If you can hold the Camera for multiple Seasons, those discounted Photos add up quickly. The Camera passes to whoever takes a Photo at the Vista, so other players can take it from you. At the end of each Season, the Camera holder gets a free Photo opportunity at the reduced cost — do not forget this. Some Gear cards also let you take Photos outside the Vista. If you combine the Camera with Photo Gear, you can generate 2-3 Photos per Season at minimal cost. Over 4 Seasons, that is 8-12 points from Photos alone.

walkthrough.title: Year Card Strategy
walkthrough.text: Your Year card is a secret Personal Bonus worth 2 or 3 points at game end. Some Year cards reward visiting a certain number of Parks with specific symbols. Others reward taking a target number of Photos. And the Year of Plenty rewards having 12 or 18 of the same token symbol across your visited Parks. Let your Year card guide your overall strategy without obsessing over it. If your Year card aligns naturally with your play — great, that is 2-3 free points. If it requires you to twist your strategy into knots, it may be better to focus on other scoring and accept 0 from the Year card.

walkthrough.title: Timing Trail End
walkthrough.text: When to send your Hikers to Trail End is one of the most important decisions in PARKS. The first Hiker to Reserve a Park gains the First Hiker Marker (1 point at game end and first turn next Season). The first Hiker to Buy Gear gets a Sunshine discount. But arriving early means your Hikers leave the Trail sooner, giving opponents more room to collect resources. Conversely, if you dawdle on the Trail too long, you might be the last Hiker — forced to skip straight to Trail End. The ideal balance: get one Hiker to Trail End early for the bonus, then let your second Hiker collect resources steadily before the Season ends.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't rush the Trail — skipped Sites are lost resources forever
Don't waste your Campfire on unoccupied Sites
Don't hoard tokens — convert them into Park visits and Photos
Check available Park costs before moving; collect tokens that match
Watch the 12-token limit — discard feels terrible

Later Seasons have longer Trails (up to 9 Sites) — more collection room

Relights automatically when a Hiker reaches Trail End
2-player: blocking is rare; 4-5 players: Campfire timing is critical
Can also share a Site with your own Hiker (costs Campfire too)

Year card may reward specific Park symbols — build toward those Parks
Wildlife tokens are wild for Parks and Photos — extremely valuable
Lodge lets you swap 2 tokens for 2 different ones — great for fixing your supply

summary.title: Gear Investment
bullets: Buy Gear early (Season 1-2) for maximum ongoing value
Discount Gear (Maps, Compasses, Journals) reduce Park visit costs
Token-swap Gear (Sunscreen, Rain Gear) give flexibility
First Hiker to Buy Gear each Season gets 1-Sunshine discount

summary.title: The Camera and Photo Strategy
bullets: Photos: 1 point each; Camera reduces cost from 2 tokens to 1
Camera passes to whoever takes a Photo at the Vista
End of Season: Camera holder gets optional reduced-cost Photo
Camera + Photo Gear = 2-3 Photos/Season = 8-12 points over the game

summary.title: Year Card Strategy
bullets: Year cards award 2-3 bonus points at game end
Let it guide strategy naturally — don't twist your game for 2 points
Year of Plenty: 12 or 18 of the same symbol across visited Parks
If alignment is easy, free points; if not, focus elsewhere

summary.title: Timing Trail End
bullets: First to Reserve a Park gains First Hiker Marker (1 pt + first turn next Season)
First to Buy Gear gets 1-Sunshine discount
Arriving too early = fewer resources; too late = forced to Trail End
Ideal: one Hiker early for bonus, second Hiker collects resources steadily

walkthrough.title: The Resource Engine Approach
walkthrough.text: In PARKS, the closest thing to an engine is combining Gear cards, Canteens, and the Camera for compounding value each Season. The Resource Engine strategy focuses on buying discount Gear early — Maps, Compasses, and Journals — that reduce the token cost of visiting Parks. With 2-3 discount Gear cards, a Park that normally costs 4 tokens might only cost 1 or 2. This lets you visit higher-point Parks that other players cannot afford. The key is investing Sunshine tokens in Gear during Seasons 1 and 2, even though it feels expensive. By Seasons 3 and 4, your discounts pay for themselves many times over.

walkthrough.title: Building the Resource Engine
walkthrough.text: Start by prioritizing the Valley (2 Sunshine) and collecting Weather tokens to build Sunshine for Gear purchases. In Season 1, buy one discount Gear card during your first Trail End visit. In Season 2, try to buy a second. From Season 2 onward, you should be visiting at least one Park per Season at a steep discount. Combine discounts with Wildlife tokens for maximum flexibility. If a Park costs 1 Forest, 1 Mountain, 1 Sunshine, and your Gear discounts Forest and Mountain, you only need 1 Sunshine — or 1 Wildlife — to visit it.

walkthrough.title: Camera Control Strategy
walkthrough.text: The Camera is one of the highest-value assets in the game. Holding it across multiple Seasons generates 1-point Photos at half price (1 token instead of 2), and you get an extra Photo opportunity at the end of each Season. The Camera Control strategy focuses on claiming and keeping the Camera. Visit the Vista early each Season to take a Photo and claim the Camera. Then defend it — since only the Vista can transfer the Camera, you need to either visit the Vista before opponents or block it with your Hiker. Over 4 Seasons, Camera control can generate 6-10 Photos, worth 6-10 points.

walkthrough.title: Maximizing Camera Value
walkthrough.text: Pair Camera control with Photo-related Gear cards for even more value. Some Gear lets you take Photos at Sites other than the Vista. Combined with the Camera's reduced cost, every Photo is cheap. Also watch for Year cards that reward Photos — if your Year card says 'Take 4 Photos' and you have the Camera plus Photo Gear, you will hit that target easily. Remember: end-of-Season Photos are free actions that many players forget. Never skip them if you hold the Camera.

walkthrough.title: Wildlife Token Strategy
walkthrough.text: Wildlife tokens are the most powerful resource in PARKS because they substitute for any token type when visiting Parks or taking Photos. The Wildlife Token strategy revolves around visiting the Wildlife Trail Site every Season, using the Lodge to create Wildlife tokens, and hoarding them for big Park visits. A hand of 3-4 Wildlife tokens lets you visit almost any Park regardless of its token requirements. This is especially strong in the late game when specific token types are scarce and competition for Sites is fierce.

walkthrough.title: The Canteen Chain
walkthrough.text: Canteens are easy to overlook, but chaining them together can generate significant bonus value. Each time you gain Water on a turn, you can fill one or more Canteens. Some Canteens grant extra tokens, some let you take actions, and some help you reach Trail End faster. The Canteen Chain strategy means picking up extra Canteens at the Vista and using Water-rich Sites (Ocean, Weather tokens) to fill multiple Canteens in a single turn. A well-timed Ocean visit with 2 Weather Water tokens could fill 3-4 Canteens at once. This burst of bonus actions can swing an entire Season.

walkthrough.title: First Hiker Marker Control
walkthrough.text: The First Hiker Marker is worth 1 point at game end, but its real value is going first each Season. Going first means you have first pick of Weather tokens, first access to unoccupied Sites, and you set the pace of the Trail. To control it, you need to be the first player to Reserve a Park at Trail End each Season. This means getting at least one Hiker to Trail End quickly — which trades off against collecting resources. The ideal play is to rush one Hiker to Trail End for the Marker while your second Hiker collects resources at a normal pace.

walkthrough.title: Blocking and Denial
walkthrough.text: PARKS has significant interaction through blocking. When your Hiker occupies a Site, other players must use their Campfire to visit it. In a 4-5 player game, blocking becomes a core strategy. Identify which Sites your opponents need — if someone is collecting for a specific Park, sit on the Wildlife or Lodge Site to deny them the tokens they need. You can also force an opponent to waste their Campfire by occupying a critical Site early. Once their Campfire is spent, they are locked out of any occupied Site for the rest of the Season.

walkthrough.title: Season Pacing and the Last Hiker Rule
walkthrough.text: The Last Hiker rule is a powerful strategic lever. When only one Hiker remains on the Trail, that player must move directly to Trail End. This means you can force an opponent to skip Sites by getting all other Hikers off the Trail. In a 2-player game, if you get both your Hikers to Trail End while your opponent still has one on the Trail, their remaining Hiker is forced to Trail End immediately — potentially skipping 3-4 Sites. Time this carefully. Rush both Hikers to Trail End when your opponent has one Hiker deep in the Trail to deny them maximum resources.

walkthrough.title: Season-by-Season Priorities
walkthrough.text: Season 1 (6 Sites): Focus on resource collection and buying your first Gear card. The Trail is short, so plan Hiker movement carefully — every Site matters. Season 2 (7 Sites): Your Gear should start paying off. Visit a Park and consider a second Gear purchase. Start thinking about your Year card goal. Season 3 (8 Sites): The Trail is long enough for both Hikers to collect heavily. This is your highest-output Season — visit 1-2 Parks and take Photos if you have the Camera. Season 4 (9 Sites): Final push. Visit as many Parks as possible, take remaining Photos, and ensure you hit your Year card goal if it is within reach. The First Hiker Marker and end-of-Season Photo are your last scoring opportunities.

summary.title: The Resource Engine Approach
bullets: Buy discount Gear (Maps, Compasses, Journals) in Seasons 1-2
Discounts stack — a 4-token Park might only cost 1-2 tokens
Investment pays off by Seasons 3-4 with cheaper Park visits
Combine with Wildlife for maximum flexibility

summary.title: Building the Resource Engine
bullets: Prioritize Valley (2 Sunshine) and Weather tokens for Gear purchases
Buy 1 discount Gear in Season 1, try for a 2nd in Season 2
Gear discounts + Wildlife = visit almost any Park cheaply
From Season 2 onward, visit at least 1 Park per Season

summary.title: Camera Control Strategy
bullets: Hold Camera across multiple Seasons for half-price Photos (1 token each)
Visit Vista early each Season to claim/keep Camera
End-of-Season Photo is free — never skip it with Camera in hand
Camera control = 6-10 Photos over 4 Seasons (6-10 points)

summary.title: Maximizing Camera Value
bullets: Pair Camera with Photo-related Gear for even cheaper Photos
Year cards rewarding Photos become easy targets
Defend Camera by visiting Vista before opponents
Every Photo is 1 point — they add up fast

summary.title: Wildlife Token Strategy
bullets: Wildlife = wild for Parks and Photos, the most flexible token
Visit Wildlife Site every Season; use Lodge to create more
3-4 Wildlife tokens can visit almost any Park regardless of cost
Strongest in late game when specific token types are scarce

summary.title: The Canteen Chain
bullets: Pick up extra Canteens at the Vista for more bonus actions

A single turn can trigger 3-4 Canteen bonuses in a burst

summary.title: First Hiker Marker Control

Reserve a Park first at Trail End to claim it
Rush one Hiker to Trail End, let second Hiker collect resources
Going first lets you set the pace and claim key Sites

summary.title: Blocking and Denial

Most impactful in 4-5 player games where the Trail is crowded

Rush both Hikers to Trail End to force opponents to skip Sites

summary.title: Season-by-Season Priorities
bullets: Season 1 (6 Sites): collect resources, buy first Gear
Season 2 (7 Sites): Gear pays off, visit a Park, consider second Gear
Season 3 (8 Sites): highest output — visit 1-2 Parks, take Photos
Season 4 (9 Sites): final push — maximize Parks, Photos, Year card goal

entries.definition: Each player controls 2 Hikers of their chosen color. On your turn, move one Hiker forward down the Trail to an unoccupied Site and perform its action. Both Hikers start on the Trailhead each Season.

Each player has 1 Campfire matching their Hiker color. Flip it to extinguished to share a Site occupied by another Hiker (including your own). Relights automatically when one of your Hikers reaches Trail End. One use per Season until relit.

Given to the player to the right of the First Hiker at game start. Reduces Photo cost from 2 tokens to 1. Transfers to whoever takes a Photo at the Vista. At end of each Season, the Camera holder may take 1 Photo at reduced cost.
An enamel pin given to the most recent real-life hiker at game start. Worth 1 point at game end. Goes first each Season. Claimed by the first player to Reserve a Park at Trail End each Season.
The starting tile on the far left of the Trail. All Hikers begin each Season here. Not considered a Trail Site — it has no action and no Weather token is placed on it.
The tile at the far right of the Trail. Not a Trail Site. When a Hiker arrives, the player relights their Campfire and chooses one of three areas: Reserve a Park, Buy Gear, or Visit a Park.
The tiles between the Trailhead and Trail End that form the hiking path. Each Site has a specific action. Sites are divided into Basic Sites and Advanced Sites (marked with an asterisk).
One of the 5 standard Trail Sites (Forest, Mountain, Valley, Ocean, Vista) plus the Waterfall in 4-5 player games. These form the core of every Trail.

One of 4 Trail Sites marked with an asterisk (Wildlife, Lodge, Lookout, River). One is added each Season, making the Trail longer and more strategic. They offer more powerful or complex actions than Basic Sites.
Basic Trail Site. Action: gain 1 Forest token from the supply.
Basic Trail Site. Action: gain 1 Mountain token from the supply.
Basic Trail Site. Action: gain 2 Sunshine tokens from the supply.
Basic Trail Site. Action: gain 2 Water tokens from the supply.
Basic Trail Site. Action: draw 1 Canteen card from the Canteen deck, OR turn in 2 tokens to take a Photo and gain the Camera (1 token if you already hold the Camera).
Basic Trail Site used only in 4-5 player games. Action: gain 1 Sunshine and 1 Water token from the supply.
Advanced Trail Site. Action: turn in any 1 token from your supply to gain 1 Wildlife token from the supply.

Advanced Trail Site. Action: turn in any 2 tokens from your supply and gain any 2 other tokens from the supply (excluding Wildlife). The gained tokens cannot match the types turned in. You may gain 2 of the same type as long as you did not turn in that type.
Advanced Trail Site. Action: Reserve or Visit a Park, OR Buy a Gear card — the same three options available at Trail End. This lets you take Trail End actions without leaving the Trail.

Advanced Trail Site. Action: spend 1 Water token to copy the action of another Trail Site that is currently occupied by any Hiker. Does not count as visiting that Site (so it does not trigger Site-specific Gear). Tokens gained still benefit from Season Effects.
One of the 5 resource token types. 15 in the supply. Represented by a green tree icon. Required for many Park visits.
One of the 5 resource token types. 15 in the supply. Represented by a gray mountain icon. Required for many Park visits.
One of the 5 resource token types. 30 in the supply. Represented by a yellow sun icon. Used to visit Parks and to buy Gear cards.
One of the 5 resource token types. 30 in the supply. Represented by a blue water drop icon. Used to visit Parks, fill Canteens, and activate the River Site.
A special wild resource. 12 in the supply, each with a unique animal shape. Can substitute for any other token when visiting Parks or taking Photos. Cannot be used in Canteens or to buy Gear.
Players may hold a maximum of 12 tokens at any time. If you exceed 12 at the end of your turn, you must discard tokens down to 12.
A brown square icon on cards that denotes any token may be used. The number inside specifies how many tokens are required.

Shown on the bottom right of each Season card. Determines which bonus tokens are placed on the Trail at the start of the Season. Tokens are placed starting at the 2nd Trail Site, one per Site, repeating the pattern until Trail End.
A special rule shown on the Season card that modifies gameplay for that round. Effects can add tokens to Parks, change resource gains, or provide bonuses for specific actions during the Season.

A Season Effect that adds 1 Sunshine or Water token on top of each of the 3 available Park cards at the start of the Season. When a player Visits or Reserves that Park, they also gain the token (can be used immediately toward the Park or a Canteen). New Parks revealed later do not receive tokens.
A Season Effect example where gaining certain tokens triggers additional bonus gains. Season Effects always apply to all players equally for the entire Season.

Tarot-sized cards (70x120mm) representing national parks. 48 in the deck. Each shows a point value, token cost to visit, the park name, state location, established date, and featured artist. 3 are face up on the board at all times.

Smaller cards (50x75mm) representing hiking equipment. 36 in the deck. Cost Sunshine to buy. All have ongoing abilities; some also have a one-time instant action on purchase. 3 are face up on the board at all times.

Smaller cards (50x75mm) representing water containers. 15 in the deck. Each player starts with 1. Gained at the Vista or from certain Gear. Filled with Water gained on the current turn to trigger a bonus action. Emptied at end of Season.

Smaller cards (50x75mm) representing personal goals. 12 in the deck. Each player keeps 1 as a secret Personal Bonus. Awards 2 or 3 points at game end based on conditions like Park symbols visited or Photos taken. First game: deal only 1 per player.
Smaller cards (50x75mm) that define each round. 10 in the deck. Each shows a Season Effect and Weather Pattern. Revealed one at a time at the start of each Season.

Worth 1 point each at game end. 28 in the supply. Taken at the Vista (cost: 2 tokens, or 1 with Camera) or via certain Gear cards. Camera holder gets an extra Photo opportunity at the end of each Season.

Trail End action. Choose 1 of the 3 face-up Parks or draw from the deck. Place it face up horizontally in front of you. Reserved Parks can be visited on a future turn at Trail End or the Lookout. The first player to Reserve a Park each Season gains the First Hiker Marker.

Trail End action (also available at the Lookout). Pay the token cost shown on a face-up Park or one of your reserved Parks. Place the visited Park vertically in your score area. Refill the board slot from the Parks deck.

Trail End action (also available at the Lookout). Choose 1 of the 3 face-up Gear cards and pay its Sunshine cost. First Hiker gets a 1-Sunshine discount (first 2 in 4+ player games). Place Gear face up in front of you; use its ongoing ability immediately.
A Gear card with an ongoing ability that discounts visiting Parks by reducing the cost of a specific token type. Multiple discount Gear cards stack on a single Park visit.
A Gear card that discounts visiting Parks by reducing the Mountain or Forest token cost. Effects are cumulative with other discount Gear.
A Gear card that discounts visiting Parks by reducing the cost of a specific token type. A token type on a Park can never be reduced below zero.
A Gear card that lets you substitute Sunshine tokens for another token type when visiting Parks. Applies to the token symbols on the Park card, not to discounts from other Gear.
A Gear card that lets you substitute Water tokens for another token type when visiting Parks. Applies to the token symbols on the Park card, not to discounts from other Gear.
A Gear card that lets you take a free Water token from the supply when visiting a specified Site and place it into a Canteen to activate it.
Some Gear cards have an instant one-time action triggered immediately upon purchase, in addition to their ongoing ability. Marked on the card separately from the ongoing ability.
All Gear cards have an ongoing ability that remains active for the rest of the game after purchase. These include token discounts, token swaps, and bonus actions at specific Sites.

The Year card kept by each player during setup. Awards 2 or 3 bonus points at game end based on a personal goal such as visiting Parks with specific symbols, taking a certain number of Photos, or collecting token symbols across visited Parks.

A specific Year card. Awards points for having 12 (2 pts) or 18 (3 pts) of the same token symbol across all of your visited Parks. The symbol can be any of the four types (Forest, Mountain, Sunshine, Water).
A Trail Site where another Hiker (including your own) is currently standing. You cannot visit an occupied Site unless you use your Campfire. Multiple Hikers can be at Trail End without restriction.
When only one Hiker remains on the Trail (all others are at Trail End), that player must move their Hiker directly to Trail End and choose a Trail End action. This ends the Season.

Place a Water token gained on the current turn onto a Canteen card instead of into your supply. Resolve the Canteen's bonus action immediately. You cannot fill Canteens with Water from a previous turn. Multiple Canteens can be filled on one turn.

When all Hikers reach Trail End: Camera holder may take a Photo. Empty all Canteens. Return Hikers to Trailhead. Rebuild Trail with 1 new Advanced Site (Trail grows longer). Reveal new Season and apply Weather Pattern. First Hiker Marker holder goes first.
The main game board that folds into three sections. Holds slots for 3 Park cards (top), 3 Gear cards (bottom), the Parks deck, Gear deck, Canteen deck, and Season deck.
Two trays included in the game, each holding all token types and Photos. Placed on either side of the board for easy access. The supply for all players.
The shared pool of tokens and Photos available to all players, stored in the two token trays. Tokens are taken from and returned to the supply during gameplay.
The area in front of each player where visited Park cards are placed vertically. Points on these cards are tallied during final scoring.

A Park card placed horizontally in front of a player after being reserved at Trail End. Can be visited on a future turn at Trail End or the Lookout by paying its token cost. Kept separate from visited Parks until scored.
At Trail End, in games of 3 or fewer players, the first Hiker to Buy Gear gets a 1-Sunshine discount. In 4+ player games, the first 2 Hikers each get the discount. Marked on the Trail End tile.
There are 5 basic resource types: Forest (green tree), Mountain (gray peak), Sunshine (yellow sun), Water (blue drop), and Wildlife (animal shapes, wild). Each has distinct uses and availability.

score_tracker.title: PARKS Score Tracker

categories.name: Parks
categories.description: Sum the point values printed on all Park cards you visited during the game.
categories.scoring: Variable — each Park card shows its own point value

categories.name: Photos
categories.description: Count all Photos you collected during the game.

categories.name: Personal Bonus (Year Card)
categories.description: Reveal your Year card and check whether you met your personal goal.
categories.scoring: 0, 2, or 3 points depending on goal completion

categories.name: First Hiker Marker
categories.description: The player holding the First Hiker Marker at game end scores a bonus point.

score_tracker.tiebreaker: Most Parks visited. If still tied, victory is shared.
